context: Monday June 29. BTC $59,954, XRP $1.044, SUI $0.685, HBAR $0.0711. F&G 12 (Extreme Fear), -6 from yesterday. Post-PCE stabilization: Core PCE came in HOT (0.3% m/m / 3.4% YoY, Jun 25) — confirmed Warsh's hawkish dot-plot from June 17. BTC flushed to $58K on Jun 26 ($1.26B liquidations) and is now holding $59,954, but volume is extremely compressed ($19.69B vs $44B on PCE day) — this is Monday pre-event consolidation, not a confirmed bottom. This week is the most macro-loaded of Q2: Warsh speaks Wednesday Jul 1 09:00 ET (first public remarks post-FOMC — market will parse every word for dovish nuance), then NFP Thursday Jul 2 08:30 ET (moved from Friday due to July 4 holiday). Jobs below 150K = Warsh's rate-hold becomes politically untenable = pivot narrative re-ignites. Jobs above 200K = hawkish case fully confirmed = $58K re-test. XRP is the highest-conviction setup: CLARITY Act is one Senate floor vote away ($1.044 now), and any Democrat 'yes' announcement produces an independent XRP rally decoupled from BTC/NFP direction. Section 604 (ethics provisions) is the specific sticking point; July 4 window tight; August recess is the final realistic deadline. SUI: avoid before Jul 1 token unlock (13.72M tokens / $9.31M tomorrow). HBAR: approaching $0.065-$0.072 OTE zone after -9.74% weekly loss — not yet; wait for sweep + reclaim confirmation. No new BTC longs before Warsh speaks.

tickers.thesis: BTC holding the $59-60K zone after the June 26 flush to $58K ($1.26B in liquidations — largest single-day wipe since the FOMC reaction). PCE came in HOT (0.3% m/m / 3.4% YoY, highest core since Oct 2023) confirming Warsh's hawkish dot-plot thesis. Price has stabilized at $59,954 but the structure is fragile: volume extremely compressed ($19.69B vs $44B on June 25 = Monday pre-event positioning), F&G still at 12 (Extreme Fear, -6 from yesterday). This week has two sequential catalysts: Warsh speaks Wednesday Jul 1 (first public remarks post-FOMC — any dovish nuance on supply-driven PCE or jobs cooling = short-cover relief), then NFP Thursday Jul 2 8:30 ET (moved from Friday due to July 4 holiday — the week's real binary). Weak NFP (<150K jobs) = pivot narrative re-ignites. Strong NFP = Warsh's hawkish case strengthens, $58K re-test likely. No new longs before NFP resolves.

tickers.thesis: XRP -7.76% on the week — now at $1.044, down from $1.22 (June 16) and $1.08 (June 25). $1.00 is the psychological floor and the line of the trade. CLARITY Act is the macro-independent re-rating catalyst: passed House 294-134, Senate Banking 15-9, now on Senate floor calendar. Hagerty's target was July 4 window but Section 604 (ethics provisions for crypto software) is the specific sticking point. ~7 Democratic votes still needed to beat 60-vote filibuster threshold. August recess (starts Aug ~10) is the realistic final window. Any Democrat 'yes' announcement this week = independent XRP rally regardless of BTC / NFP direction. XRP ETF cumulative inflow still above $1.44B — ETF bid provides a floor. Below $1.00: no tactical long until reclaim. Above $1.05: watch $1.10.

insights.title: Sentiment deepening: F&G 12 (Extreme Fear), Δ -6 vs yesterday
insights.body: Crypto Fear & Greed Index printed 12 (Extreme Fear) at 06:24Z — -6 vs yesterday's reading. F&G under 30 historically aligns with bounce-watch zones; under 20 with capitulation. Combined with the recent price action and live headlines (see LIVE FEED), the regime is risk-off until a sweep-and-reclaim candle prints. No new longs until F&G stops falling AND BTC reclaims its 24h high.

insights.title: NFP Thursday: the week's binary, not Friday
insights.body: Non-Farm Payrolls are Thursday Jul 2 at 08:30 ET — moved from Friday due to the July 4 holiday. This is the most important data point of the week. Below 150K jobs: Warsh's hawkish stance becomes politically untenable, pivot narrative re-prices, risk-on. Above 200K jobs: hawkish case confirmed, $58K BTC re-test, XRP $1.00 floor tested. Consensus is ~180K; anything within 20K of consensus = flat reaction. Market enters Wednesday with a maximum-fear posture (F&G 12) — the asymmetric surprise is to the upside if NFP disappoints.

insights.title: Warsh Wednesday: first public remarks post-FOMC are a pre-NFP signal
insights.body: Fed Chair Warsh speaks Wednesday Jul 1 at 09:00 ET — his first public remarks since the hawkish June 17 FOMC where he eliminated forward guidance and pushed the median 2026 dot to 3.8% (implying a possible hike). Markets will listen for: (1) any acknowledgment that PCE was energy/supply-driven rather than demand-driven, (2) any softening of the no-forward-guidance stance, (3) comments on labor market softening threshold. Even one dovish sentence = short-cover rally. A doubling-down = NFP carries even more weight.

insights.title: XRP: highest-conviction setup of the four — CLARITY is the wildcard
insights.body: XRP at $1.044 is the highest-conviction trade this week. The CLARITY Act floor vote could be called at any time before August recess — each Democrat 'yes' commitment is a flash rally regardless of BTC or NFP direction. Section 604 (crypto software ethics provision) is the specific sticking point. ETF cumulative inflow at $1.44B+ provides an institutional floor. Risk/reward: risk $0.10 (to $0.94 stop) for $0.176 to $1.22 TP1 if CLARITY clears. The macro macro-independent driver is what makes this unusual.

insights.title: SUI Jul 1 unlock: small absolute but wrong time to front-run
insights.body: 13.72M SUI tokens ($9.31M at $0.685) unlock tomorrow July 1. This is small relative to the $2.76B market cap (~0.34% dilution) but the risk is not the size — it is the timing. In a risk-off tape (F&G 12, BTC compressed, Warsh speaks Wednesday) any incremental sell pressure can cascade. The unlock is the worst possible condition for a long entry. Wait for the unlock to absorb (Tuesday/Wednesday) before evaluating the $0.672-$0.685 OTE.

insights.title: BTC at $59,954: testing the PCE flush low, but volume is non-confirming
insights.body: BTC held the $58,935 24h low and the $58K June 26 flush level for 4 days now. That is structural base-building. The problem is volume: $19.69B today vs $44B on PCE day (June 25). Low volume at support is a fragile floor, not confirmed accumulation. A confirmed floor requires either (a) NFP miss (demand-driven re-pricing) or (b) Warsh softening tone, not just price holding. Don't read the hold as a buy signal until one of those triggers fires.

hidden_signals.body: Fear & Greed at 12 (Extreme Fear), Δ -6 vs yesterday — sub-30 zones historically align with bounce setups but require a sweep-and-reclaim candle to confirm

BTC volume today ($19.69B) is under 45% of PCE-day volume ($44B, Jun 25). When spot volume collapses to this level at a support zone it signals one of two things: genuine accumulation by smart money (who doesn't advertise) OR a lack of buyers at any price above. The ambiguity resolves on NFP Thursday. Watch for volume picking up Wednesday around Warsh's speech as the first tell.

CLARITY Act Section 604 is specifically about whether crypto software developers become regulated as 'financial institutions' under BSA/AML rules. The Democrat holdouts (Ossoff, Padilla, Smith) have flagged this as their specific concern. If any of them announce an amendment or a 'yes' vote this week, Axios and Politico break it 30-60 min before the Senate floor announcement. Monitor those sources in real time, not aggregators.

SUI token unlock Jul 1 (13.72M tokens) is the visible overhang. The less-visible one: the ongoing vesting schedule has been releasing 55.31M+ SUI per quarter in early 2026. The cumulative float expansion has been suppressing price since February. Post-unlock the next large tranche is in Q3 — check Sui Foundation's token economics page for exact schedule before sizing any swing position.

F&G at 12 (Extreme Fear) for three consecutive readings (Jun 25, Jun 28, Jun 29). Historically, F&G spending more than 3 days below 15 without a bounce has preceded either (a) a capitulation wick that produces the real bottom, or (b) a prolonged sub-20 grind (July 2023 type action). BTC at $55K is the capitulation case; $60K hold + NFP miss is the bounce case. The ambiguity is why you size small here.

Warsh's June 17 'no forward guidance' stance was not a technical policy change — it was a philosophical reset. The Fed no longer telegraphs moves. This structurally increases volatility on every data print because markets can no longer pre-position on Fed language. NFP Thursday is the first major data print since that reset took effect. Expect outsized moves vs. historical NFP reactions — both up AND down.

BTC dominance has been rising during the post-FOMC risk-off: HBAR -9.74% week, XRP -7.76%, BTC -6.35%. Alts always bleed faster in macro fear regimes. This means the BTC/$59,954 'stability' masks deeper alt damage. HBAR has now lost 13% since June 16 — approaching a level (below $0.072) where Governing Council enterprise buyers historically provided a bid. That support zone has not been tested since February 2026.

PCE came in at 3.4% YoY — the highest core reading since October 2023. A key detail not widely covered: the energy component (Middle East supply constraint via shipping disruption) accounts for ~0.5pp of the 3.4%. Strip that and core is closer to 2.9%. If Warsh acknowledges this supply-vs-demand distinction Wednesday, it creates intellectual space for the 2026 dot-plot median to come down without a policy reversal. That is the most underpriced scenario in the market right now.

HBAR's Governing Council now has 31 members (FedEx added February 2026). But council membership commits governance participation, not purchase obligations. The $10B+ in RWA settlements flowing through Hedera is real institutional adoption — but that transaction flow does not require buying HBAR spot (fees are tiny, settled in HBAR but immediately converted). The enterprise thesis is structurally sound but does NOT translate into price support on its own in a risk-off macro environment.
